_comment: World B usage-aware cascade. Routing policy lives in routing-policy.json; this file supplies the executable cascade classes. A chunk's executor intent maps to a class here; cascade-dispatch.sh walks that class's role ladder, resolves roles per host via harness-profile.json, checks live headroom (usage-probe.sh) + the quality_floor, dispatches the best available rung, and on a cap error fires the Airlift Tier-1 checkpoint and DESCENDS. DeepSeek/OpenRouter mechanical-agent routing in dm-review reads the same routing policy.

class_from_kind._comment: kind (manifest) -> cascade class. Detailed task-fit lives in routing-policy.json; config/docs go OpenRouter-primary, logic AND integration go Codex-primary (integration moved off Claude with the 2026-08 plan change), ui stays Claude-primary for visual/Live Wires judgment.

quality_rank._comment: Global model->score; enforces quality_floor regardless of which role surfaced the model. Higher = better. Bare slugs (gpt-5.6-sol) are the Codex-native names; openai/-prefixed slugs are the same models via the OpenRouter wrapper -- both carry the same score.
quality_rank._comment_gpt56: GPT-5.6 (Sol/Terra/Luna, GA 2026-07-09) supersedes GPT-5.5. Sol is same price and same 1.05M context as gpt-5.5 ($5/$30 per 1M) and a newer generation, so it strictly dominates and takes the frontier head; gpt-5.5 is retained one rung down as a fallback (it is also the rung an older codex-cli can still reach, via the orchestrator's Native Model Descent -- cascade-dispatch.sh itself never walks past the first floor-clearing model of a `kind: native` role). Terra ($2.50/$15) slots between opus-4.8 and sonnet-5. Luna ($1/$6) is the cheapest OpenAI rung but is NOT a cheap_api model -- its output tokens cost 3.4x z-ai/glm-5.2 ($1.76) and 6.9x deepseek-v4-pro ($0.87), so it lives at the frontier_api tail, never at the cheap head.
quality_rank._comment_codex_mini: gpt-5.1-codex-mini keeps a rank so a stray re-add scores above the codex floor rather than silently resolving to 0, but it appears in NO ladder: `codex exec --model gpt-5.1-codex-mini` returns HTTP 400 'not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account', and the codex host runs on the ChatGPT sub. Do not re-add it to harness-profile.json.
quality_rank._comment_kimi_k3: Kimi K3 (moonshotai/kimi-k3, Moonshot AI, GA 2026-07-16) is the open-weight frontier: 2.8T-param MoE (16/896 experts active, Kimi Delta Attention + attention residuals), native vision, 1M context, $3/$15 with $0.30 cache hits. AA intelligence index 57 -- comparable to Opus 4.8/GPT-5.5, behind Sol/Fable; GDPval-AA 1687 (ahead of Opus 4.8's 1600). Rank 91 (Terra tie) is a positional estimate that discounts the benches for Moonshot's own stated UX gap, ~20 TPS output, and max-only reasoning effort -- revise when the technical report and SWE-bench numbers land. frontier_api ONLY: $15/M output is 8.5x z-ai/glm-5.2, so it never appears on cheap_api/bulk_api/openrouter_exec. It is the only vision-capable model on the OpenRouter rail (GLM-5.2 has none). PRIVACY: sole OpenRouter provider until the weights ship (planned 2026-07-27) is Moonshot's first-party API with no ZDR variant. Privacy is DEMOTED per the policy block (Quality > Price > Speed > Privacy), so K3 is fully live by default; only under opt-in OPENROUTER_ZDR=1 does it fall through as a per-model failure until a Western no-log host carries the weights.

codex._comment: Primary executor = Codex (kind logic/config). Sub first; when Codex caps, descend to a cheap open model via OpenRouter BEFORE falling all the way to Claude -- this is the key cost win and the generalization of the merged single fallback.

openrouter._comment: Primary executor = OpenRouter for config/docs/mechanical-logic. The openrouter_exec rung writes, verifies, commits, and emits usage; Codex is secondary and Claude native judgment is last.

claude._comment: Primary executor = Claude (kind ui -- needs visual/Live Wires judgment; integration moved to the codex class 2026-08). Claude native first, but the plan is now PRO (downgraded from Max, effective 2026-08): the rail is scarce, judgment-only spend that caps fast. When Pro caps, cross to the Codex Pro 20x sub (premium_sub) BEFORE paying per-token frontier API rates. Never drops to a cheap model -- quality floor 80 protects ui work.

policy._comment_headroom: Treat a sub/native rail as exhausted once probed remaining quota drops below this -- hand off BEFORE the hard wall (mirrors Airlift's early-warning trip).
policy._comment_reentry: When an exhausted sub rail's resets_at passes it re-enters at its original ladder position (flat-rate and free again -- prefer quality).
policy._comment_subscriptions: Subscription rails (effective 2026-08): Claude plan downgrades Max -> Pro; Codex moves to Pro 20x. Optimize Codex-first: the codex premium_sub rail is the deep flat-rate workhorse, the Claude native rail is demoted to Pro-level acceptance (Sonnet-class daily driver, scarce quota, judgment-only). fable and opus are likely plan-unavailable on Pro -- their native entries stay (inert, RC-64 degrades to the next model) but sonnet now leads the claude-code native_judgment ladder.
policy._comment_fable: fable (Claude Fable 5, Mythos-class, rank 97) is PLAN-CONDITIONAL: available only when the user's current plan carries it. On Claude Pro (2026-08) it is almost certainly off-plan and sits behind sonnet in the claude-code native_judgment ladder as an inert upgrade-path entry -- no probe can detect plan-level availability, so if a native directive names a model the plan does not carry, the orchestrator dispatches the next model in the native list (see execution-orchestrator RC-64 rule). Keep the entry when fable is off-plan; it is inert until availability returns.
policy._comment_native_host_constraint: gpt-5.6-sol outranks fable (98 vs 97) but leads native_judgment only on the CODEX host -- claude-code's `kind: native` accepts Claude aliases only. Rationale and failure mode: docs/opus-4-8-tuning.md (Fable vs GPT-5.6 Sol).
policy.privacy: DEMOTED (2026-07-18, user directive): model selection priority is Quality > Price > Speed > Provider privacy. OPENROUTER_ZDR is OPT-IN, never a default pin -- Chinese first-party hosting (Moonshot/DeepSeek/Z.AI) is acceptable by default. Set OPENROUTER_ZDR=1 per-call only for genuinely sensitive material (client code under NDA, credentials-adjacent diffs); when set, a model with no eligible ZDR provider (e.g. moonshotai/kimi-k3 pre-weights) is a per-model failure and the ladder walks on.
policy._comment_headroom_unknown: usage-probe state 'unknown' is treated as HAS-headroom (fail-open): a stale/unverified parser prefers dispatch + reactive cap detection over a false 'capped' that needlessly skips a healthy rail. The codex rail has a reactive cap detector (usage-limit string match) in cascade-dispatch.sh; the claude native rail relies on the orchestrator's own cap handling.
policy._comment_agentic_seam: openrouter_exec is the agentic OpenRouter rung for config/docs/mechanical-logic. Wrapper rungs (cheap_api/frontier_api) remain single-turn analysis/text generation and are still invalid for complex logic/ui/integration implementation.
